fundamentals.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Spotify Technology S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides audio streaming subscription services worldwide. It operates in two segments, Premium and Ad-Supported. The Premium segment offers online and offline streaming access to its catalog of music and podcasts, including video, lossless music, and audiobooks in select markets through subscription offerings primarily sold directly to end users and partners. The Ad-Supported segment provides limited on-demand online access to its catalog of music and online and offline access to its catalog of podcasts on computers, tablets, mobile devices, and other smart devices. The company also offers sales, distribution and marketing, contract research and development, and customer and other support services. Spotify Technology S.A. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

judgment.summary: Strong growth in earnings and revenue, but high valuation multiples and significant debt present tradeoffs.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 18.0% (EPS 3.45 vs 2.92); next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth 220.4% YoY, indicating strong recent performance.
Analyst growth estimates are high for the current quarter (769.9%) and fiscal year (22.4%).
Operating margin of 15.8% and profit margin of 15.4% show healthy profitability.
Return on equity at 38.0% and return on assets at 11.6% indicate effective use of capital.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 34.36 and Forward P/E of 27.83 suggest a high valuation.
PEG ratio of 1.79 indicates that growth may not fully justify the current stock price.
Debt/Equity ratio of 5.94 is high, suggesting significant leverage.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 6.03 is also on the higher side.

news.paragraph: Spotify (SPOT) is gaining attention following a collaboration with Universal Music Group on AI remixes, while also being highlighted as a potential investment by Steve Cohen. Despite a recent 3.2% dip and some analyst views suggesting it's overvalued, Bank of America raised its price target, indicating a split opinion among watchers. The company's strong earnings growth and analyst estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year suggest potential upside.
